title: Into the Wasteland - a solo post-apocalyptic card game
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YBMZINlORaw

Daniel (@dungeondive) examines Into the Wasteland, a delightfully minimalist post-apocalyptic survival game that nails theme and artwork while keeping the rules light enough to fit in a tiny box. You're managing a fleet of vehicles across three days of scavenging: picking up civilians and resources, weighing constant binary choices about which resources to grab, and deciding when to gamble by entering high-risk facilities. The card art—designed like a comic book—immediately drew Daniel in, and the game delivers on that gritty Mad Max aesthetic.

The core loop is pure resource management with a press-your-luck core: each day you work through the entire wasteland deck, drawing encounter cards that force you to spend ammo, supplies, or accept damage. Combat is simple but tense—roll dice, try to beat the enemy's target number, trade resources to add more dice. Specialist cards add some asymmetry. Daniel's critique is fair: the game is almost there, but it needs one more layer of meaningful decision-making. Vehicle-specific abilities or mixable driver cards with their own powers would dramatically increase depth and variability without complicating the rules. As it stands, Into the Wasteland is a charming, elegant introduction to survival mechanics—just shy of being a full recommend.

----

What's a small, simple game that grabbed you by its theme alone? Would more character customization push Into the Wasteland into your collection?
